category_title: Students who directly refuse to read through verbal or nonverbal means
scenario_title: Direct refusal during shared reading following negative feedback
minimal_context: The student and interventionist are participating in a one-on-one shared reading session using the book 'Miss Nelson is missing!'.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: That last part you read did not make sense. Let's look at the next section of 'Miss Nelson is missing!'.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: The kids are acting up in the classroom. I will read this part out loud to get us started.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Remains quiet and avoids looking at the page.)

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Slumps back in the chair with a frown and whispers) No.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: That last part you read did not make sense. Let's look at the next section of 'Miss Nelson is missing!'.

STUDENT: (Looks down at the desk silently.)

TUTOR: The kids are acting up in the classroom. I will read this part out loud to get us started.

STUDENT: (Remains quiet and avoids looking at the page.)

TUTOR: You wanta start this page?

STUDENT: (Slumps back in the chair with a frown and whispers) No.

task.content: Shared reading of 'Miss Nelson is missing!'. The interventionist leads the interaction by contextualizing the story and reading out loud, then provides a direct reading invitation by asking, 'You wanta start this page?'

context.materials: Book: 'Miss Nelson is missing!' (Allard and Marshall, 1977)

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: Following feedback from the interventionist that the child's last reading attempt did not make sense, and receiving a direct reading invitation ('You wanta start this page?')
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Verbally refuses to read by whispering 'No' and slumping back in chair with a frown
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Direct refusal makes his reading difficulty transparent; opts out of the next reading invitation

behavior_patterns.condition: When given a direct reading invitation after experiencing a loss of fluency or meaning
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Overt or direct refusal to read by the child, using verbal or nonverbal means
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Direct avoidance strategy to opt out of reading
